note: Single copper coin, strong bluish cast; enhanced crop shows globe lower-left and big numeral 1 with CENT — classic 1 cent common face.
Seed 1 shows a clear 5 with globe. Uncircled rightmost coin is a 1 euro: clear numeral 1, gold ring / silver center (greenish cast). Size ratios consistent (1c < 5c < 1euro).
Rotated crop reads 10 EURO CENT clearly. Coin gold-toned under teal/blue cast.

Seed 0 shows clear 5 + globe; seed 1 shows 1 + globe and is smallest (r ratio 0.71 vs 5c). Seed 2 very worn, numeral unreadable; size ratio to 5c (0.86) matches 2cent (18.75/21.25=0.88). All copper family, dark bluish cast.

Seed 2 reads 50 EURO CENT clearly. Seeds 0 and 1 are very dark/tarnished, bimetal ring contrast not visible, but both are LARGER than the 50c (r approx 72 and 70 vs 66) — only the 2 euro (25.75mm) exceeds 50c (24.25mm), so both labeled 2euro by size. Seed 0 partially occluded by the 50c.

Complete set laid out in two rows: top 1c,2c,5c,10c; bottom 20c,50c,1euro,2euro. Radii anchored to the 2euro match all nominal diameters. Seed 4 = 1euro: gold ring (tarnished brown) with darker silver center, bimetal boundary visible, size between 50c and 2euro. Seed 0 = 2euro: silver ring (appears pale tan from cast), clear numeral 2, largest coin. Seeds 2 (50) and 3 (20) numerals clearly readable.
